75.reason: [Incorrect allocation size: sizeof(line) is the size of a pointer, not the struct. Should be sizeof(struct xd).]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: sizeof(line) is the size of a pointer, not the struct. Should be sizeof(struct xd).]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: sizeof(line) is the size of a pointer, not the struct. Should be sizeof(struct xd) or sizeof(*c).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  sizeof(line) returns the size of a pointer (4 or 8 bytes), not the size of the struct. This causes heap corruption when accessing struct members. It should be sizeof(struct xd) or sizeof(*c).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  sizeof(line) allocates memory for a pointer type, not the struct itself. It should be sizeof(struct xd) or sizeof(*c).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect allocation size: sizeof(line) is the size of a pointer, not the struct. Should be sizeof(struct xd).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [malloc(sizeof(line)) allocates size of a pointer, not the struct; should be sizeof(struct xd)]

84.reason: The merging loop only considers connections to the head node; it never attempts to merge two other nodes that might be connectable. This can cause missing merges.

58.reason: In this function, the parameter r actually receives q1 (the previous node), not the node being merged. It incorrectly adds the count of the previous node instead of the merged node. It should be p->co += t->co; where t is the node being merged.

86.reason: Declaring a variable inside the for-loop initializer is not allowed in C89/C90 standards, which causes a compilation error. Declare 'r' before the loop.

103.reason: In C89/C90, variable declarations must appear at the beginning of a block. Placing it after executable statements causes a compilation error. Move this declaration to the top of main.
